latest.title: New investment in Audrey AI
latest.content:
16 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6606A
Mindflair PLC
16 April 2026
16 April 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
New investment in AI-driven financial auditing platform
Audrey AI
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology, is pleased to announce that Sure Valley Ventures Fund III ("SVV3"), in which Mindflair holds an interest, has made a new investment in Audrey A
I, a Dublin-based startup
company developing
an AI platform for financial auditors.
Audrey AI has raised
US
$1.8 million (€1.5 million) in a pre-seed funding round led by Sure Valley Ventures and Delta Partners, with participation from Enterprise Ireland, Donnchadh Casey (ex-CEO Calypso), Conor Jones (ex-CBO Wayflyer), and several former auditors.
The funding w
ill be used to scale the Audrey AI platform and expand its engineering and audit specialist teams as the company grows its presence across Ireland, the UK and international markets.
Audrey AI is addressing the global financial auditing market, valued at over
US
$100 billion, which
still
remains heavily reliant on manual processes. While general
purpose AI tools often struggle with the complex tabular data and evidence workflows require
d by the
auditing
profession, Audrey AI's agentic system is designed to automate the most time
intensive aspects of an audit engagement, specifically evidence gathering and testing.
In pilot
engagement
with
T
op
10 and
T
op
20 audit firms,
reduced time spen
t
on
client data collection, validation and tests of detail
by over 85%
, while simultaneously improving audit quality. The system is designed to learn each firm's specific audit methodology
, increasing in value as it is used.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
Audrey AI represents a compelling application of agentic AI in a high-value, highly regulated sector. By automating the manual and repetitive workflows that have historically burdened the auditing profession, Audrey AI provides a critical solution to talen
t shortages and margin pressures. We believe the company is well-positioned to become a
key
infrastructure layer for the next generation of audit firms
."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.
Enquiries:
Mindflair plc
Nicholas Lee, Director
Tel: +44 (0) 20 3368 8961
Nominated Adviser
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP
Liam Murray / Ludovico Lazzaretti

Tel: +44 (0) 20 7469 0936
Notes to Editors
Mindflair plc (AIM: MFAI) is an investment company providing investors with access to a portfolio of next generation technology businesses focused on AI with significant growth potential. The Company is building an investment portfolio of high-tech businesses focused on Artificial Intelligence, across such areas as Internet of Things, Cyber Security, Machine Learning, Immersive Technologies and Big Data, which the Board believes demonstrate evidence of traction and the potential for exponential growth, due to increasing global demand for development in these sectors. For further information, visit:
http://www.mindflair.tech/
.
Audrey AI is a Dublin-based startup building AI purpose-built for financial auditors. Its agentic automation platform orchestrates complex client data and applies audit procedures end-to-end, enabling firms to scale capacity and improve audit quality. For more information, visit:
www.tryaudrey.ai

latest.title: New investment in Agentic payments company
latest.content:
14 Apr 2026 08:44
RNS Number : 3720A
Mindflair PLC
14 April 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
New
i
nvestment in Agentic
p
ayments
i
nfrastructure
c
ompany
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology is pleased to announce that Sure Valley Ventures Fund II ("SVV2"), in which Mindflair holds an interest, has made a new investment in Ralio Limi
ted ("Ralio"), a UK-based payments infrastructure company building technology tailored for B2B AI agents.
Ralio has raised $2.5 million in pre-seed funding in a round led by Sure Valley Ventures, with participation from Seed X, Love Ventures, Plug and Pla
y, rule30, Adeline Arts and Science, Endurance Ventures, Campus Fund, fintech business angel Alan Morgan and continued support from Antler. The funding will be used to further develop Ralio's core platform and expand its engineering team in London.
Ralio
is building a payments infrastructure
system
purpose-built for autonomous B2B AI systems. As AI agents become increasingly capable of executing financial workflows independently, existing payment systems remain designed primarily for human users, relying o
n manual authentication, approval processes and human-centric fraud controls. Ralio's platform is designed to address this gap by embedding guardrails, identity verification and audit trails directly into agentic transactions, enabling AI systems to safely
and accountably move money across payment
rails
including bank transfers, cards and stablecoins.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
Ralio is addressing an important infrastructure challenge at the intersection of AI and financial services. As AI agents become more capable of operating autonomously, the ability for them to transact safely, compliantly and with full accountability will b
e a critical enabling layer for enterprise adoption. We believe Ralio is well positioned in
this
emerging category with significant long-term potential.
"
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

latest.title: Literal Labs wins AI award
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7049Y
Mindflair PLC
31 March 2026
31 March 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Literal Labs
w
ins
AI
a
ward
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI"), related technology, is pleased to
note
that Literal Labs, one of the Company's portfolio companies, has been named a winner in the AI-Research & Innovation Award
category at the Global AI Excellence Award 2025.
Literal Labs was recognised in the AI-Research & Innovation Award category as part of the Global AI Excellence Award 2025 programme.
The Global AI Excellence Award
recognises organisations advancing AI across a number of categories including integration, innovation, ethics, data, hardware and generative AI. Literal Labs is developing highly efficient logic-based AI techno
logy designed to enable faster, lower-power inference, with a particular focus on delivering AI that can run effectively on standard processors and edge devices. This recognition reflects the strength of Literal Labs' technology and its potential commercia
l relevance across a range of AI applications.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
We are delighted to see Literal Labs recognised with the AI-Research & Innovation Award at the Global AI Excellence Award 2025. This award is a
n
endorsement of the company's technical innovation and reinforces our belief in the strength of Mindflair's AI-focused portfolio. Literal Labs is addressing important challenges around AI efficiency, speed and deployability, and we look forward to supportin
g its continued progress."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

For further information, visit: http://www.mindflair.tech/

Literal Labs
is a UK-based artificial intelligence company pioneering logic-based AI, with technology designed to deliver faster, more energy-efficient and more explainable inference than traditional neural networks, while running efficiently on standard MCUs and CPUs without the need for GPUs. www.literal-labs.ai

latest.title: Interview with the CEO of Stylus
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4911W
Mindflair PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Interview with the CEO of Stylus
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology, is pleased to share a video of an interview featuring Dominic Bristow, founder and CEO of LearnCycle Limited, trading as Stylus Education ("Stylus"), a portfolio company of Mindflair.
Stylus, an AI education technology company, has reported strong commercial momentum, with 50 new schools added to its customer base in January 2026 alone, almost exactly one year after signing its first customer. The interview highlights the company's continued progress as it expands the adoption of its AI-powered assessment platform within the education sector.
Dominic Bristow explains that the company is addressing a significant gap in the education market through an AI-powered end-to-end marking service for schools. He highlights Stylus's ability to improve the consistency and transparency of assessment, and notes that the company is now in its second year of working with the UK Department for Education through an innovation contract.
Watch
Stylus
founder and CEO Dominic Bristow
's interview
here:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3uS7TE-6K5o
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

Stylus Education is an AI education technology company focused on improving the assessment of student writing. company_30s.what_they_do: MFAI Ltd is a venture‑capital vehicle that builds and backs early‑stage artificial‑intelligence businesses.  Its portfolio includes sales‑automation AI, secure inference engines, and other software that helps companies harness data and machine learning.
company_30s.why_they_matter: AI is reshaping every industry—from retail to defence—and the capital that fuels these startups is highly sought after.  By spotting and funding the next generation of AI tools, MFAI positions itself to capture upside from a market that is growing into the hundreds of billions of dollars.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in its growth phase, raising capital and adding new portfolio companies.  It has recently announced several funding rounds and a webinar aimed at explaining its investment strategy and potential returns.  The stock is trading far below its historical highs, signalling a prolonged sell‑off.

rns_narrative: The company has been actively adding new AI investments, most notably a sales‑execution platform and a secure inference startup.  These moves show a continued focus on high‑margin, data‑centric businesses.
A series of investor‑webinar announcements and a scheduled ‘realisation’ event suggest the firm is preparing to return capital to shareholders, which could act as a catalyst.
The narrative also indicates a steady pace of portfolio development without any signs of distress or regulatory problems.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – the community is waiting for the webinar and potential distribution.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls see the upcoming webinar as a chance to hear about new high‑growth deals and a possible dividend‑style payout.  They expect the announcement to lift sentiment and price.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the firm’s recent investments may not generate returns quickly enough and that the distribution could be delayed or smaller than expected, leaving the stock stuck in a low‑valuation trap.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been largely neutral, with a few bullish posts about the webinar.  No significant negative commentary has emerged, suggesting that the market is not yet fully aware of any downside.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no public search interest.  This lack of attention means that any positive news will hit the market with a surprise factor.

near_term_catalysts: The 24th March investor webinar – a chance to announce a distribution or new flagship investment.
Any announcement of a ‘realisation’ (capital return) that follows the December event, which could provide an immediate cash‑in for shareholders.
A spike in trading volume that aligns with a price break above a key support level (the 52‑week low).
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include: 1) A positive statement about the size or timing of the distribution; 2) A clear timeline for next funding round or exit; 3) A sudden uptick in daily volume that exceeds the 10‑day moving average.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep‑crash recovery.  The APEX score indicates a tight price compression, which often precedes a breakout.  Confirmation will come from a sustained move above the recent low and a volume surge.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the webinar confirms a distribution or a clear path to cash returns, the stock could rebound by 70‑100% from its current trough.  A 50% recovery would already double the price, delivering a risk‑to‑reward ratio of roughly 1:3 when factoring in the deep‑crash origin.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the firm fails to deliver a distribution or the new investments fail to generate traction, the stock could remain stuck in a low‑valuation zone.  The worst case is a further 20‑30% decline if the market loses confidence in the fund’s strategy.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 70‑100% from the trough.  Potential downside: limited to the current price level, as the stock cannot fall below zero.  The asymmetry is highly favourable for a contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the webinar if the speaker confirms a distribution or a clear exit plan.  Allocate 10‑15% of the portfolio to a long position, using a stop 5% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position and watch for a volume spike of at least 1.5× the average daily volume.  If that occurs with a price break above the 52‑week low, consider adding to the position.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified, so a pass is unlikely.  Stay bullish on the deep‑crash setup.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MFAI is a deep‑crashed AI‑venture fund with a quiet market; a forthcoming webinar could unlock a distribution and trigger a 70‑plus percent rebound, making it a high‑reward contrarian play.
